item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't work for me, at least. Too many fuzzy references, too many confusing for the sake of confusing bits of text. The idea seems too simple to be considered well thought up and creative and at the same time too complex to be grasped in one read. That's what I consider the writing uncanny valley that every writer should avoid ("The machine is sending people to a location WORSE than the original BAD location it was programmed to send people and wants to die! BUT… [really difficult-to-follow-stuff] and [obscure references]!")

I kind of see what you were going for but I really don't like it. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorts' and Abettik's comment cleared up some points about this article for me, but I wouldn't have got it by reading the skip alone. No vote.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Alright, so this might be cheating a little, but I reached out to UraniumEmpire and they confirmed that the "Unseelie Queen" makes an appearance in The Last Things Dr. Darryl Loyd Ever Did, in Chronological Order As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

Do you really need a sapient containment cell? My brother in Site 56 breathes life free of anomalies, and that Unseelie Queen has resigned to her fate as its prisoner. D-Class? That "Carnival" of yours bags more per month than I've taken in my lifetime. Because you Protect, not Destroy? Come off it, Master told me what you did with that cup.

To this:

Do you really need a sapient containment cell? My brother in Site 56 breathes life free of anomalies, and that Unseelie Queen has resigned to her fate as its prisoner. D-Class? That "Carnival" of yours bags more per month than I've taken in my lifetime. Because you Protect, not Destroy? Come off it, Master told me what you did with that cup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knows you were going for confused, but I really don't enjoy things I can't follow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally don't fully understand what it suppposed to look like…the structure, I mean.
However, I do like it being self-aware. I do like the fact that it is suggestive of there being a traitor within the Foundation, and that the computer is trying to request being destroyed.

Seems interesting. It needs work, but with the correct adjustments this will become a very good SCP. Nice work, but more detail on exactly what this SCP is would make things a bit easier to understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was waffling on this one, but the outright mention of "collapsibles", which ostensibly don't exist in-universe (even here they're used for related documents of various clearance levels), tipped it over to -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Re: why it's OK with collecting them for experimentation but not for [REDACTED]: it's just that bad. Or it thought it was collecting them for some much nicer reason.

Re: collecting D-class, I'd assume the Foundation has a considerable level of control over the prison systems, runs most/all of the execution chambers and several prisons, and doesn't need to fool anyone to collect them, beyond perhaps the prison wardens who think they're sending people to be executed rather than to be experimented on.

The only part I don't get is why the dates are in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one got a little too cute for its own good. I don't think the central concept justified the addition of the experience-dampening format screw.

With regards to the sapient machine's feelings, I'm just not really buying it. Like, it was totally fine feeding people to the Foundation when it thought the D-Class were being used for horrific human experimentation, but now all of a sudden it has a conscience because they're being used for something else? I might be able to get behind this if more were made of the machine being told that it was liberating people unjustly imprisoned for crimes they didn't commit and sending them on a beach vacation in Bermuda or something, and then it finds out they're really just breaking their legs and leaving them for 106.

Also, I'm really unable to get past the mechanics of how this works. You're just driving a death row inmate along, and all of a sudden you just decide to drop your charge off with some shadowy guys, and that's that. Condemned inmates aren't that numerous, and them just disappearing would cause a lot of problems for the Foundation in the "we'd rather operate in secret" department. Granted, that's kind of a general problem with the notion that D-Class come from this population, which isn't limited to your article. But it seems like this could have been a lot more thought out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love this one. +1, and if it was possible I'd give it a +2. I like it just that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Maybe I'm in the minority, but I didn't find this all _that_ difficult to follow. There are certainly more complicated skips. Here's my understanding:

+ Spoiler
- Hide

The Foundation funded creation of -2000 to harvest D-Class by psychically contacting the drivers of death row buses. Instead, one of the creators was really a hidden EVIL agent within the Foundation and, instead of using the death row inmates as D-Class, built -2000 to send them into a horrible hellish dimension [EXPUNGED] place. Maybe he's harvesting souls. Maybe he's feeding a giant chihuahua. Who knows.

Anyways, -2000 was sentient but no idea what was going on and happily carried out its duties, thinking it was helping the Foundation. Then we investigated, added a tracker to one of the death row people, which worked, and -2000 and some other top men learned where the D-Class are _really_ going. God knows where, we weren't told, but it's bad. At that very moment -2000 had its little meltdown, started saying its sorry, and started sending out psychic signals to anyone nearby to have them come kill it. It also gave us the message saying, "PLZ KILL ME IM DANGEROUS" in the form of that last fake skip entry.

Interesting idea, and worthy of an upvote on that alone.

Though I wish we had SOME hint as to what happens to the death rows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then it failed miserably, because an article that tries to be confusing or obfuscating needs to delicately balance the thread between compelling and repelling. There's a difference between "I don't get it, I need to read this again" and "I don't get it, what was the point of all that? Screw it, this was lame"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am really trying to see what is going on here, but not even a third scrutinizing read had helped me. Not even the dates. It has many interesting parts that aren't elaborated upon, and undeservingly at that.

Where do the D-s go to?

What was the contained SCP?

What was the device constructed for?

I would definitely like to read more about those. This didn't do much for me and thus I have to -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2000 was created to harvest D-class, and now it is aware of what it is doing and is horrified and wants to be stopped.

And apparently it is a giant red herring in the first place, diverting Foundation resources instead of adding to them by collecting D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that to be the point of the article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it, either

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm gonna fess up and say I have no idea what's going on here. This has to be the most esoteric of all the contest entries, IMO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's called Site-2000 because it's dedicated to containing SCP-2000. Some people label their sites like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh, I guess it's ok. I'm neutral as of now. But the one thing I definitely disagree with is having a site-2000. There is no way the foundation has THAT many sites in place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't make a littlest bit of sense. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno. Hm. It's not a bad concept, but the actual writeup didn't interest me a whole lot. The 'master' thing had me rolling my eyes, and the repetition of the procedures and description was extra reading for little payoff.

I do like the Unseelie thing, but not enough for me.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yet another mistake from the guys at the Foundation. I'm not really sure whether the archived descriptions of the skip or the current ones are true. The last log at the end kind of helped but it's still kind of ambiguous as to who's in control of the written document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reader comment 1
See reader Comment 2, below.
Reader comment 2
See reader Comment 3, below.
Reader comment 3
See reader Comment 4, below.
Reader comment 4
See reader Comment 5, below.


This

Reader comment 5
See reader Comment 6, below.


strikes

Reader comment 6
See reader Comment 7, below.


me

Reader comment 7
See reader Comment 8, below.


as

Reader comment 8
See reader Comment 9, below.


kinda

Reader comment 9
Seriously, just don't do collapse this much.


annoying.

I also felt that 2000-B served no purpose because you never described it in its description. Is it the computer program inside the machine? Gus, the machine's janitor? A big purple monster that drags the D-class the last 5 feet? We don't know, because you didn't say it.

Oh wait I get it, obfuscation. Hahaha. Uggggghhhh.

+1 in spite of these problems, because I liked the heart of the story. But this needs work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one. Any time I come up with a complaint, I immediately figure out why it's not a problem. Well done.

Oh yeah, is that supposed to be "red tape" in the last bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the first set of Special Containment Procedures, "Passengers may to be recruited as D-Class personnel."

However, upvoted anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the -B log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything that I thought it could be when I first saw it in chat, and more. Djoric's right, though, you might want to go through this and tighten up tone in some places, but overall it was not sufficient to prevent me from upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The part with -B, unless I am truly being thick, really doesn't do anything for the scip at all, there's a period after the date when there should be a comma, and the overall tone could use a bit more tightening around the edges.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where would those hiccups be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that's a glove across the face.

e: with extra thought, seeing this combined with Bold's entry would be utterly, unimaginably amazing.

ee: vote removed for the moment, there are some hiccups in there that need fixing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please, could you stop the noise
I'm trying to get some rest
From all the unborn chicken voices
In my head

Image from here
